title: Structural battery packs and methods related thereto
abstract: Batteries are provided that include a base including a body having a hole, and an inner wall extending away from the base and disposed around the hole. An interior of the inner wall and the hole form at least part of a through-hole extending through the battery. The battery includes an outer wall extending away from the base and disposed around the inner wall. Cells are disposed on the base and between the inner wall and the outer wall. Structural battery packs are also provided that include: a hollow elongated member including a first interior space between its first and second ends; and one or more batteries disposed around the hollow elongated member between its first and second ends. Each battery includes a through-hole. The hollow elongated member is disposed through the through-hole of each battery. Methods related to the batteries and the structural battery packs are also provided.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS What is claimed is: 1. A structural battery pack comprising: a hollow elongated member comprising a first interior space between first and second ends of the hollow elongated member; and one or more batteries disposed around the hollow elongated member between the first and second ends of the hollow elongated member; wherein each of the one or more batteries comprises a through-hole; and wherein the hollow elongated member is disposed through the through-hole of each of the one or more batteries. 2. The structural battery pack of claim 1, wherein each of the one or more batteries comprises: a base comprising a body having a hole disposed in the body; an inner wall extending away from the base and disposed around the hole, wherein an interior of the inner wall and the hole form at least part of the through-hole extending through the respective battery, and wherein the through-hole is shaped and sized to fit the hollow elongated member within the through-hole; and an outer wall extending away from the base and disposed around the inner wall; wherein a plurality of cells is disposed on the base and between the inner wall and the outer wall. 3. The structural battery pack of claim 1, further comprising a first base, wherein the hollow elongated member extends away from the first base to the first end of the hollow elongated member, wherein the first end of the hollow elongated member is distal to the first base, and wherein the one or more batteries are disposed around the hollow elongated member between the first base and the first end of the hollow elongated member. 4. The structural battery pack of claim 3, further comprising an end cap, wherein the hollow elongated member extends away from the end cap to the second end of the hollow elongated member, wherein the second end of the hollow elongated member is distal to the end cap, and wherein the one or more batteries are disposed: 

 between the end cap and the second end of the hollow elongated member; and between the end cap and the first base. 5. The structural battery pack of claim 4, further comprising a hollow elongated shell comprising a second interior space between first and second ends of the hollow elongated shell, wherein the hollow elongated shell extends away from the first base to the first end of the hollow elongated shell, wherein the hollow elongated shell extends away from the end cap to the second end of the hollow elongated shell, and wherein the batteries are disposed in the second interior space of the hollow elongated shell. 6. The structural battery pack of claim 5, further comprising one or more protective inserts disposed around the hollow elongated member, wherein the one or more protective inserts are disposed within the second interior space of the hollow elongated shell. 7. The structural battery pack of claim 6, further comprising: a first wire coupled to a first terminal of the one or more batteries, wherein the first terminal serves as a first end of voltage potential for the one or more batteries, and wherein the first wire extends through the hollow elongated member; a second wire coupled to a second terminal of the one or more batteries, wherein the second terminal serves as a second end of voltage potential for the one or more batteries; wherein the one or more batteries comprises a plurality of rechargeable lithium-ion batteries; and interconnecting bars coupled between each of the plurality of batteries such that the plurality of batteries are configured to generate the voltage potential. 8. The structural battery pack of claim 7, wherein each of the one or more batteries comprises: a second base comprising a body having a hole disposed in the body; an inner wall extending away from the second base and disposed around the hole, wherein an interior of the inner wall and the hole form at least part of the through-hole extending through the respective battery, and wherein the through-hole is shaped and sized to fit the hollow elongated member within the through-hole; an outer wall extending away from the second base and disposed around the inner wall; 

 wherein a plurality of rechargeable cells is disposed on the second base and between the inner wall and the outer wall. 9. The structural battery pack of claim 8, wherein the hollow elongated shell has a hexagonal- tube shape, and wherein the end cap and the first base have a hexagonal shape and are sized to align with the first and second ends of the hollow elongated shell, respectively. 10. The structural battery pack of claim 9, wherein the outer wall has a hexagonal -tube shape. 11. The structural battery pack of claim 3, further comprising a hollow elongated shell comprising a second interior space between first and second ends of the hollow elongated shell, wherein the hollow elongated shell extends away from the first base to the first end of the hollow elongated shell, and wherein the batteries are disposed in the second interior space of the hollow elongated shell. 12. The structural battery pack of claim 3, further comprising one or more protective inserts disposed around the hollow elongated member, wherein the one or more protective inserts are disposed around the hollow elongated member between the first base and the first end of the hollow elongated member. 13. A battery comprising: a base comprising a body having a hole disposed in the body; an inner wall extending away from the base and disposed around the hole, wherein an interior of the inner wall and the hole form at least part of a through-hole extending through the battery; and an outer wall extending away from the base and disposed around the inner wall; wherein a plurality of cells is disposed on the base and between the inner wall and the outer wall. 14. The battery of claim 13, wherein the hole is disposed in a center of the base, and wherein the outer wall has a cylindrical-tube shape and is concentrically disposed around the center of the base. 15. The battery of claim 14, wherein the hole has a circular shape, and wherein the inner wall has a cylindrical-tube shape and is concentrically disposed around the center of the base. 16. The battery of claim 15, further comprising a cover; wherein the inner and outer walls extend away from the cover; wherein the cover is disposed at a first end of the inner and outer walls; wherein the base is disposed at a second end of the inner and outer walls; wherein an interior space is formed between the base, the cover, and the inner and outer walls; and wherein the cells are rechargeable cells and disposed in the interior space. 17. The battery of claim 16, further comprising one or more cell modules disposed in the interior space, wherein the one or more cell modules comprise the cells, and wherein the cells are lithium-ion cells. 18. The battery of claim 13, wherein the hole is disposed in a center of the base, and wherein the outer wall has a hexagonal -tube shape and is concentrically disposed around the center of the base. 19. The battery of claim 18, further comprising a cover; wherein the inner and outer walls extend away from the cover; wherein the cover has a hexagonal shape and is disposed at a first end of the inner and outer walls; wherein the base is disposed at a second end of the inner and outer walls; wherein an interior space is formed between the base, the cover, and the inner and outer walls; and wherein the cells are rechargeable cells and disposed in the interior space. 20. A method of making a structural battery pack, the method comprising: providing a hollow elongated member comprising a first interior space between first and second ends of the hollow elongated member; disposing a plurality of batteries around the hollow elongated member by inserting the hollow elongated member through through-holes in each of the batteries; connecting the batteries of the plurality with interconnecting bars; and 

 coupling a first base to a first end of the hollow elongated member; wherein each of the batteries comprises: a second base comprising a body having a hole disposed in the body; an inner wall extending away from the second base and disposed around the hole, wherein an interior of the inner wall forms a through-hole extending through the respective battery; and an outer wall extending away from the second base and disposed around the inner wall; wherein a plurality of cells is disposed on the second base and between the inner wall and the outer wall. 21. The method of claim 20, further comprising disposing one or more protective inserts around the hollow elongated member. 22. The method of claim 21, further comprising: coupling a first wire to a first terminal of the one or more batteries, wherein the first terminal serves as a first end of voltage potential for the one or more batteries; extending the first wire through the hollow elongated member; coupling a second wire to a second terminal of the one or more batteries, wherein the second terminal serves as a second end of voltage potential for the one or more batteries; and coupling an end cap to the second end of the hollow elongated member; wherein the plurality of cells is rechargeable lithium-ion cells.
